intro: Pentameris humbertii, also known as the Humbert's grass, is a species of grass within the Poaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly in Africa, for its purported health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties, aiding in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, cough, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Pentameris humbertii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory and gastrointestinal disorders.
